The '''70th annual [[Berlin International Film Festival]]''' took place from 20 February to 1 March 2020.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_19976.html |title=Dec 19, 2019 Berlinale 2020: Key Visual and 70th Anniversary Events |work=Berlinale |access-date=19 December 2019 |archive-date=19 December 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191219192341/https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_19976.html }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/roberto-benigni-pinocchio-1st-titles-2020-berlin-film-fest-1263284 |title=Berlin: Roberto Benigni-Starring Live-Action ''Pinocchio'' Among First Titles for 2020 Festival |work=The Hollywood Reporter |date=17 December 2019 |access-date=19 December 2019}}</ref> It was the first under the leadership of new Berlin Film Festival board: business administration director Mariette Rissenbeek and artistic director [[Carlo Chatrian]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://variety.com/2020/film/spotlight/carlo-chatrian-leads-berlin-fest-into-new-era-1203505313/|title=Carlo Chatrian Leads Berlin Fest Into New Era|first1=Nick|last1=Vivarelli|date=17 February 2020}}</ref>

The festival opening gala was presented by actor [[Samuel Finzi]], followed by the world premiere ''[[My Salinger Year]]'', directed by [[Philippe Falardeau]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://variety.com/2020/film/news/berlin-film-festival-opens-with-my-salinger-year-subdued-atmosphere-1203510056/|title=Berlin Film Festival Opens With ''My Salinger Year'', Subdued Atmosphere |author=Ed Meza |author2=Manori Ravindran |date=21 February 2020}}</ref> The [[Golden Bear]] was awarded to the Iranian film ''[[There Is No Evil]]'', directed by [[Mohammad Rasoulof]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-51693417 |title=Berlin International Film Festival: Iranian film about executions wins top prize |work=BBC News |date=29 February 2020 |access-date=1 March 2020}}</ref>

==Juries==
[[File:Jeremy Irons - Berlin International Film Festival (Berlinale) - 2013.jpg|thumb|247x247px|[[Jeremy Irons]], Main Competition Jury President]]

===Main competition===
* [[Jeremy Irons]], British actor - Jury President<ref>{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2020/film/festivals/kenneth-lonergan-berenice-bejo-annemarie-jacir-join-berlin-jury-1203491976/ |title=Kenneth Lonergan, Bérénice Bejo, Annemarie Jacir Join Berlin Jury |work=Variety |date=4 February 2020 |access-date=4 February 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_23944.html|title=Jeremy Irons Will Be the Jury President of the Berlinale 2020|work=Berlinale|date=9 January 2019|access-date=9 January 2019|archive-date=30 January 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200130142643/https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_23944.html}}</ref>
* [[Bérénice Bejo]], French actress
* Bettina Brokemper, German producer
* [[Annemarie Jacir]], Palestinian filmmaker and poet
* [[Kenneth Lonergan]], American playwright and filmmaker
* [[Luca Marinelli]], Italian actor
* [[Kleber Mendonça Filho]], Brazilian filmmaker, producer and film critic

===Encounters===
* Shōzō Ichiyama, Japanese producer<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_36897.html|title=Encounters Jury|work=Berlinale|access-date=16 April 2020|archive-date=14 June 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200614121610/https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_36897.html}}</ref>
* Dominga Sotomayor, Chilean film director, writer and producer
* Eva Trobisch, German film director

===First Feature Award===
* Ognjen Glavonić, Serbian film director and writer<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_36850.html|title=GWFF Best First Feature Award|work=Berlinale|access-date=16 April 2020|archive-date=15 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200215214256/https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_36850.html}}</ref>
* [[Hala Lotfy]], Egyptian filmmaker, producer and founder of Hassala Films
* Gonzalo de Pedro Amatria, film scholar

===Documentary Award===
* Gerd Kroske, German filmmaker<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_36849.html|title=Berlinale Documentary Award|work=Berlinale|access-date=16 April 2020|archive-date=5 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200205153651/https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_36849.html}}</ref>
* [[Marie Losier]], French filmmaker and curator
* [[Alanis Obomsawin]], Canadian filmmaker, singer and artist

===Short Film Competition===
* Réka Bucsi, Hungarian filmmaker<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_27848.html|title=International Short Film Jury 2020|work=Berlinale|access-date=16 April 2020|archive-date=16 January 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200116143244/https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_27848.html}}</ref>
* Fatma Çolakoğlu, Turkish curator (Turkey)
* [[Lemohang Jeremiah Mosese]], Sotho filmmaker and artist

==Other sections==
The Berlinale retrospective included more than 30 films by Hollywood filmmaker [[King Vidor]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_8008.html |title=Berlinale 2020: Retrospective "King Vidor" |work=Berlinale |access-date=28 February 2020 |archive-date=28 February 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200228092402/https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_8008.html }}</ref> The homage was dedicated to [[Helen Mirren]], with Mirren awarded with the [[Honorary Golden Bear]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/press/press-releases/detail_17611.html |title=Berlinale 2020 – Homage and Honorary Golden Bear for Helen Mirren |work=Berlinale |access-date=28 February 2020}}</ref> The ''Berlinale Camera'' was awarded to [[Ulrike Ottinger]] followed by the world premiere of Ottinger's documentary ''Paris Calligrammes''.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://womenandhollywood.com/director-ulrike-ottinger-will-be-honored-at-berlinale-2020/ |title=Director Ulrike Ottinger Will Be Honored at Berlinale 2020 |work=Women and Hollywood |access-date=28 February 2020}}</ref>

The Series section, introduced in 2015,<ref>{{cite web | title=Berlinale Special & Berlinale Series | website= Berlinale | url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/festival/sections/berlinale-special-and-berlinale-series.html | access-date=11 November 2021}}</ref> is devoted to longform [[television series]]. In 2020, there were two Australian entries: ''[[Stateless (TV series)|Stateless]]'' and ''[[Mystery Road (TV series)|Mystery Road]]'' Series 2.<ref>{{cite web | last=Zhou | first=Debbie | title='People are starting to wake up': Berlin film festival spotlights Australia's 'unfinished business' | website=the Guardian | date=27 February 2020 | url=http://www.theguardian.com/film/2020/feb/28/people-are-starting-to-wake-up-berlin-film-festival-spotlights-australias-unfinished-business | access-date=22 March 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | title=World premieres for Stateless and Mystery Road at Berlin International Film Festival | website=ABC |publisher =Australian Broadcasting Corporation | date=15 January 2020 | url=https://about.abc.net.au/press-releases/world-premieres-for-stateless-and-mystery-road-at-berlin-international-film-festival/ | access-date=22 March 2020}}</ref>
[[File:MJK 74704 Mohammad and Baran Rasoulof (Golden Bear, Berlinale 2020).jpg|thumb|[[Mohammad Rasoulof]], video chatted at the award winners' press conference of the Berlinale]]

==Official Awards==
The following prizes were awarded:<ref name="14Plus">{{cite web|url=https://www.berlinale.de/en/festival/awards-and-juries/awards-generation.html|title=Prizes of the Youth Jury in the competition === Main Competition ===
* [[Golden Bear]]: ''[[There Is No Evil]]'' by [[Mohammad Rasoulof]]
* [[Silver Bear Grand Jury Prize]]: ''[[Never Rarely Sometimes Always]]'' by [[Eliza Hittman]]
* [[Silver Bear for Best Director]]: [[Hong Sang-soo]] for ''[[The Woman Who Ran]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Actress]]: [[Paula Beer]] for ''[[Undine (2020 film)|Undine]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Actor]]: [[Elio Germano]] for ''[[Hidden Away (2020 film)|Hidden Away]]''
* [[Silver Bear for Best Screenplay]]: [[Damiano and Fabio D'Innocenzo]] for ''[[Bad Tales]]''
* '''Silver Bear for Outstanding Artistic Contribution''': Jürgen Jürges for ''[[DAU. Natasha]]'' (cinematography)
* '''Silver Bear 70th Berlinale''': ''[[Delete History]]'' by [[Benoît Delépine]] and [[Gustave Kervern]]

=== Short Film Competition ===
* [[Golden Bear for Best Short Film]]: Keisha Rae Witherspoon for ''T''

=== Best First Feature ===
* Camilo Restrepo for ''Los conductos''

=== Encounters ===
* '''Best Film''': ''[[The Works and Days|The Works and Days (of Tayoko Shiojiri in the Shiotani Basin)]]'' by C.W. Winter & Anders Edström
* '''Best Director''': [[Cristi Puiu]] for ''Malmkrog''
* '''Special Jury Award''': ''The Trouble with Being Born'' by Sandra Wollner
** '''Special Mention''': ''Isabella'' by Matías Piñeiro

=== Panorama ===
* '''Audience Award''':
** '''1st Place''': ''Father'' by [[Srdan Golubović]]
** '''2nd Place''': ''[[No Hard Feelings (2020 film)|No Hard Feelings]]'' by Faraz Shariat
** '''3rd Place''': ''[[Hope (2019 film)|Hope]]'' by Maria Sødahl
* '''Audience Award – Documentaries''':
** '''1st Place''': ''[[Welcome to Chechnya]]'' by [[David France (writer)|David France]]
** '''2nd Place''': ''Saudi Runaway'' by Susanne Regina Meures
** '''3rd Place''': ''Little Girl'' by [[Sébastien Lifshitz]]

=== Generation ===

==== Generation 14Plus ====
* '''Crystal Bear for the Best Film''': ''Our Lady of the Nile'' by [[Atiq Rahimi]]
** '''Special Mention''': ''[[White Riot (film)|White Riot]]'' by Rubika Shah
* '''Crystal Bear for the Best Short Film''': ''[[Mutts (film)|Mutts]]'' by [[Halima Ouardiri]]
** '''Special Mention''': ''[[Goodbye Golovin]]'' by Mathieu Grimard
* '''Grand Prix of the Generation 14Plus International Jury for the Best Film''': ''[[My Name Is Baghdad]]'' by Caru Alves de Souza
** '''Special Mention''': ''Voices in the Wind'' by [[Nobuhiro Suwa]]
* '''Special Prize of the Generation 14plus International Jury for the Best Short Film''': ''Mutts'' by Halima Ouardiri
** '''Special Mention''': ''White Winged Horse'' by Mahyar Mandegar

==== Generation KPlus ====
* '''Crystal Bear for Best Film''': ''Sweet Thing'' by [[Alexandre Rockwell]]
** '''Special Mention''': ''H Is for Happiness'' by [[John Sheedy (director)|John Sheedy]]
* '''Crystal Bear for the Best Short Film''': ''The Name of the Son'' by Martina Matzkin
** '''Special Mention''': ''Miss'' by Amira Géhanne Khalfallah
* '''The Grand Prix of the International Jury in Generation Kplus for the Best Film''': ''The Wolves'' by Samuel Kishi Leopo
** '''Special Mention''':
*** ''[[Cuties]]'' by [[Maïmouna Doucouré]]
*** ''Mum, Mum, Mum'' by Sol Berruezo Pichon-Rivière
* '''Special Prize of the International Jury in Generation Kplus for the Best Short Film''': ''The Name of the Son'' by Martina Matzkin
** '''Special Mention''': ''The Kites'' by Seyed Payam Hosseini

* '''Best Documentary/Essay Film''': ''[[If It Were Love]]'' by Patric Chiha
* '''Best Short Film''': ''Playback'' by Agustina Comedi
* '''Special Jury Award''': ''[[Days (2020 film)|Days]]'' by [[Tsai Ming-liang]]

* '''Competition''': ''[[Undine (2020 film)|Undine]]'' by [[Christian Petzold (director)|Christian Petzold]]
* '''Panorama''': ''[[Mogul Mowgli]]'' by [[Bassam Tariq]]

** '''Special Mention''': ''Overtures'' by The Living and the Dead Ensemble
* '''Encounters''': ''The Metamorphosis of Birds'' by Catarina Vasconcelos

=== [[Prize of the Ecumenical Jury]] ===

* '''Panorama''': ''Father'' by [[Srdan Golubović]]
